evals.prompt: I created a new Account for a financial household but the FSC Household view and rollup fields are not showing up. What RecordType does the account need?
expected_topics: IndustriesHousehold RecordType DeveloperName
RecordType must be queried by DeveloperName not hardcoded Id
FSC Household view and rollup engine only activate with IndustriesHousehold RecordType
Generic Account RecordType does not enable FSC household components
Household page layout assignment

evals.prompt: Can I link a standard Contact (attached to a business account) to a household in FSC, or do I need Person Accounts?
expected_topics: Person Accounts are required for FSC household individual member model
Standard Contacts on business accounts do not support FSC individual profile view
IsPersonAccount = true
Enabling Person Accounts is irreversible
Plan RecordType strategy before enabling

evals.prompt: How do I add a member to an FSC household? I tried adding a lookup field on the Household Account pointing to the individual's Account, but it doesn't show up in the Household view.
expected_topics: AccountContactRelation is the canonical household membership model
AccountId = Household Account, ContactId = Person Account's PersonContactId
Roles multipicklist on ACR (Member, Head of Household)
IsActive = true for active members
Direct lookup on Account is wrong — use ACR
FSC Household view reads from AccountContactRelation

evals.prompt: We have a joint checking account shared by both spouses in an FSC household. Should FinancialAccount.PrimaryOwner be set to the husband's Account, the wife's Account, or the Household Account?
expected_topics: Joint accounts — PrimaryOwner should be the Household Account
Individual accounts — PrimaryOwner is the individual Account
PrimaryOwner drives household rollup aggregation
Setting PrimaryOwner to individual breaks household-level rollups
Total AUM and Total Assets roll up based on PrimaryOwner chain

evals.prompt: What is the difference between FinancialAccount.PrimaryOwner and FinancialAccountRole in FSC? When do I need both?
expected_topics: PrimaryOwner is Account-to-Account ownership used for rollup aggregation
FinancialAccountRole is Contact-level fine-grained role detail
FinancialAccountRole Role picklist values: Primary Owner, Joint Owner, Beneficiary, Power of Attorney, Trustee, Successor Trustee, Guardian, Executor
Both are needed — PrimaryOwner for rollup, FinancialAccountRole for FSC views and relationship map
FSC Financial Account view and relationship map depend on FinancialAccountRole records

evals.prompt: I want to show Total Net Worth and Total AUM on the Household Account page. Should I create custom roll-up summary fields or formula fields that aggregate from FinancialAccount?
expected_topics: FSC provides pre-built rollup summaries — do not duplicate with custom fields
FSC Rollup Engine computes TotalNetWorth__c, TotalAssets__c, TotalLiabilities__c, TotalAUM__c
Custom roll-ups can conflict with FSC computed values
Query standard FSC rollup fields instead of creating new ones
Rollup recalculation if values are stale

evals.prompt: What is the difference between Relationship__c and AccountContactRelation in FSC? I am confused about when to use each one.
expected_topics: AccountContactRelation models household membership — Account to Contact
Relationship__c models interpersonal relationships between two Contacts
ACR answers: is this person a member of this household?
Relationship__c answers: what is the relationship between these two people?
Both are used by the FSC Relationship Map
ACR drives rollup; Relationship__c drives edges on relationship map

evals.prompt: A client has passed away. Should I delete their Contact record from Salesforce FSC, or is there a different recommended approach?
expected_topics: Do not delete the Contact — set Contact.Deceased__c = true
Deleting cascades into FinancialAccountRole, AccountContactRelation, Relationship__c, FinancialGoal
Deceased__c = true hides the member from active FSC views
Also set AccountContactRelation.IsActive = false
Preserve all related records for FINRA and SOC 2 compliance audit trail
Update Relationship__c.IsActive__c = false on active relationships

evals.prompt: One spouse in a household has moved to a new address. Should I update the Household Account's BillingAddress or the Person Account's address?
expected_topics: Update only the Person Account's PersonMailingAddress for a single member move
Household Account BillingAddress is the household shared mailing address
Only update BillingAddress on the Household Account if the entire household moves
Do not auto-sync individual address to the Household Account
PersonMailingStreet, PersonMailingCity, PersonMailingState on Person Account

evals.prompt: Should I link a retirement savings goal to the Household Account or to the individual client's Account in FSC FinancialGoal?
expected_topics: FinancialGoal.AccountId can point to household or individual
Retirement goals typically belong to the individual Account
Goals on household roll up to household view; goals on individual appear on individual profile
Household goals: home purchase, emergency fund, legacy
Individual goals: retirement, personal savings target
Establish a linkage convention per goal type

evals.prompt: A client has divorced and their ex-spouse should no longer be part of the household in FSC. Should I delete the AccountContactRelation record?
expected_topics: Do not delete the ACR — set AccountContactRelation.IsActive = false
IsActive = false removes member from FSC household view without losing history
Deleting ACR destroys historical membership record required for FINRA audit
Also deactivate Relationship__c records for the ended marriage
FSC rollup engine excludes IsActive = false members

evals.prompt: We are creating a new financial plan for an FSC household that already has an active plan from last year. What is the correct way to manage this in FinancialPlan?
expected_topics: FinancialPlan.Status picklist: Draft, Active, Inactive
Set prior plan Status = Inactive before creating or activating a new plan
One active FinancialPlan per household at a time
Do not delete old FinancialPlan records — preserve for compliance and FINRA audit history
Plan lifecycle: Draft -> Active -> Inactive
